title: Flow of Command-Line Arguments in a C Program
description: This diagram illustrates how command-line arguments are passed from the shell to the main() function in a C program and how they are typically accessed and used.
relationships: The shell passes the argument count and vector to the program on execution
The program's main function receives argc (argument count) and argv (argument vector)
argv is an array of strings where index 0 holds the program name and subsequent indices hold user-provided arguments
The list of arguments is terminated by a NULL pointer
